Notice Of Special Motion And Motion To Strike Under Ccp Sec 425.16(Anti-Slapp)
Off Calendar Jan-12-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DOUGLAS W. ALLAN, Tel No. (415) 439-8889 appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Dotun Oyenuga, individually and dba Analysis Solutions Consultants;
Atty. CARLOS JATO, Tel No. (415) 771-6174 appearing for the Cross-Defendant Edward Oguejiofor.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT EDWARD OGUEJIOFOR'S Special Motion To Strike Under CCP Sec 425.16 (Anti-Slapp) is denied. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an anti-SLAPP motion under CCP sec. 425.16. "First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity'If the court finds such a showing has been made'it then determines whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67.) Cross-Defendant has not established that the cause of action arises from protected activity. The statements were not made in an "official proceeding." Although Labor Code sec. 1726 requires an "awarding body" to investigate potential prevailing wage violations, the Department of Industrial Relations defines "awarding body" as the "department, board, authority, officer or agent awarding a contract for public work." (Cross-Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. C, Printout from DIR webpage). The plain language of this definition establishes that the "awarding body" is the governmental entity that awards the contract. There is no language that indicates that this definition should be extended to include general contractors who hire subcontractors. Further, this case is distinguishable from Kibler v. Northern Inyo County Local Hospital Dist. (2006) 39 Cal.4th 192) as there is no indication that any investigation into payment of prevailing wages had commenced. Additionally, the statements do not relate to "to the substantive issues in the litigation and [were not] directed to persons having some interest in the litigation." (Neville v. Chudacoff (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1255, 1266.) "Section 425.16 does not accord anti-SLAPP protection to suits arising from any act having any connection, however remote, with an official proceeding. The statements or writings in question must occur in connection with an issue under consideration or review in the proceeding." (Paul v. Friedman, 95 Cal. App. 4th 853, 866.) The underlying complaint in this matter asserts causes of action for wage and hour violations. The cross-complaint alleges that Cross-Defendant informed the general contractor that he was an independent contractor who anticipated filing a mechanic's lien on the project and that Cross-Complainant was hostile to the general contractor's employees. These statements do not relate to the substantive issues raised in the wage and hour causes of action. Because Cross-Defendant has not established that the cross-complaint arises out of protected activity, the motion is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
